linked_context.page_description: Claude Code /dmg skill — persistent memory + session sync (git, docs, memory in one command) - responsiblparty/claude-dmg-skill
linked_context.page_excerpt:
A Claude Code slash command that ends every session by committing dirty repos, refreshing documentation, and updating a persistent memory system — so the next session starts with full context.

When you build things with Claude Code, context resets every session. You re-explain your project layout. You re-describe decisions you already made. Claude doesn't know what changed yesterday.

The other problem: after a productive session you have uncommitted work, stale docs, and no record of what you learned.

hacker_news.title: Show HN: Llmbuffer – Python library for cache-optimized LLM conversation history
hacker_news.author: scottmp10
hacker_news.summary: LLM conversation buffer with cache optimization and dynamic context. - scottpurdy/llmbuffer

linked_context.page_title: GitHub - scottpurdy/llmbuffer: LLM conversation buffer with cache optimization and dynamic context.
linked_context.page_description: LLM conversation buffer with cache optimization and dynamic context. - scottpurdy/llmbuffer
linked_context.page_excerpt:
Most LLM applications naively concatenate their system prompt, conversation history, and any dynamic context into a single message list — and rebuild it from scratch every turn. This works, but it leaves significant money and latency on the table by constantly invalidating the provider's prompt cache.

llmbuffer assembles your messages in the order that maximises cache reuse, manages the boundary between stable and changing content, and handles compaction when history grows too long — all without you having to think about it.

The static system prompt and committed conversation history form a byte-stable prefix that is never mutated or re-ordered across turns. The frequently-changing parts — RAG results, timestamps, in-flight tool calls — live at the end where they can't invalidate the prefix.

hacker_news.content_context:
I was not getting good cache utilization when including dynamic context in agent threads. After a lot of experimentation, I found a good pattern that minimizes how often long lived conversation history gets modified while still supporting dynamic context. It has flexible hooks for doing things like truncating or summarizing tool outputs when transitioning messages to the long term history. And I'm seeing >>90% of tokens hitting the cache for my agents despite including a lot of dynamic user context.

There are a wide range of agent prompting strategies so I'd love to hear where this library works well and where there are patterns that don't fit well into the current API! Live music generation at 25 frames per second, on the iPhone in your pocket. Ten unbroken minutes of 48 kHz stereo without melting your phone. Zero dropouts. Even on a 2020 iPhone 12 Pro. And the GPU never wakes up.

This is Google DeepMind's Magenta RealTime 2 ( mrt2_small , 230M parameters), surgically altered into three Core ML graphs and placed on the silicon each one wants. The temporal transformer holds p99 ≈ 14 ms on the iPhone's Neural Engine against a 40 ms frame budget. hacker_news.content_context:
Last Thursday, Deepmind released Magenta Realtime 2 , an open source music generation model. They said it could run on Mac, but not iPhone.

As a v̵i̵b̵e̵ ̵c̵o̵d̵i̵n̵g̵ ̵a̵d̵d̵i̵c̵t̵ agentic AI maxxi and person who has melted iPhones before (link at bottom), I took that as a personal challenge and made it my weekend project.

On Saturday, I got it to run for 10min straight on an iPhone 12 Pro from 2020 without melting the phone or - shockingly - touching the GPU.

How? I chopped the model up into 5 pieces and set them each to run on different parts of Apple's system on a chip (SoC).

My past experience taught me that if you can actually leverage it, the iPhone's NPU is incredibly powerful, and power efficient. If you're doing sustained real-time generation for long periods of time on a device without a fan, you gotta use the neural engine or else you will melt the device.

See: https://accelerateordie.com/p/we-melted-iphones-for-science

hacker_news.title: Nuts – pip/NPM for Java with first-class workspaces and JDK provisioning (9y+)
hacker_news.author: thevpc
hacker_news.summary: Nuts Java Package Manager. Contribute to thevpc/nuts development by creating an account on GitHub.

linked_context.page_title: GitHub - thevpc/nuts: Nuts Java Package Manager
linked_context.page_description: Nuts Java Package Manager. Contribute to thevpc/nuts development by creating an account on GitHub.
linked_context.page_excerpt:
Website: https://thevpc.github.io/nuts Docs: Official documentation

Every major language has a package manager that lets you run things, not just build them. Python has pip , Node has npm , Rust has cargo . Java has Maven — but Maven resolves dependencies at compile time , for developers .

pip install doesn't just run a script. It installs, manages versions, isolates environments, and handles the full lifecycle. Java has never had that — until Nuts.

hacker_news.content_context:
My frustration with distributing java apps didnt show up recently. I remember having implemented my first network jar downloaded back in the 2000's because i needed applet like feature support with desktop full control. Years after, the problem is the very same. Webstart didnt really took off and the only mean i had in my projects was the ugly fatjars, including the (for me) uglier spring-boot repackaging that changes the application classloading behaviour and hence giving me by time some headackes i was not prepared for.

So basically nuts started as a response to this frustration 9 years ago, but from now i think its mature enough (used in production) to be shared, and forecebly i am more keen to need suggestions and help from fellow contributors. linked_context.page_excerpt:
A Claude Code slash command that ends every session by committing dirty repos, refreshing documentation, and updating a persistent memory system — so the next session starts with full context.

When you build things with Claude Code, context resets every session. You re-explain your project layout. You re-describe decisions you already made. Claude doesn't know what changed yesterday.

The other problem: after a productive session you have uncommitted work, stale docs, and no record of what you learned.

top_trends.title: Show HN: Llmbuffer – Python library for cache-optimized LLM conversation history
top_trends.author: scottmp10
top_trends.summary: LLM conversation buffer with cache optimization and dynamic context. - scottpurdy/llmbuffer

linked_context.page_title: GitHub - scottpurdy/llmbuffer: LLM conversation buffer with cache optimization and dynamic context.
linked_context.page_description: LLM conversation buffer with cache optimization and dynamic context. - scottpurdy/llmbuffer
linked_context.page_excerpt:
Most LLM applications naively concatenate their system prompt, conversation history, and any dynamic context into a single message list — and rebuild it from scratch every turn. This works, but it leaves significant money and latency on the table by constantly invalidating the provider's prompt cache.

llmbuffer assembles your messages in the order that maximises cache reuse, manages the boundary between stable and changing content, and handles compaction when history grows too long — all without you having to think about it.

The static system prompt and committed conversation history form a byte-stable prefix that is never mutated or re-ordered across turns. The frequently-changing parts — RAG results, timestamps, in-flight tool calls — live at the end where they can't invalidate the prefix.

top_trends.content_context:
I was not getting good cache utilization when including dynamic context in agent threads. After a lot of experimentation, I found a good pattern that minimizes how often long lived conversation history gets modified while still supporting dynamic context. It has flexible hooks for doing things like truncating or summarizing tool outputs when transitioning messages to the long term history. And I'm seeing >>90% of tokens hitting the cache for my agents despite including a lot of dynamic user context.

There are a wide range of agent prompting strategies so I'd love to hear where this library works well and where there are patterns that don't fit well into the current API! named_hotspots.core_details: 该项目由addyosmani等人创建，专注于为AI编码智能体提供生产级工程技能，使用Shell语言开发，当前评论数达5694条。
named_hotspots.ai_take: 该项目反映了业界对提升AI编码智能体工程化能力的迫切需求，旨在将AI辅助编程从实验阶段推向实际生产环境。
named_hotspots.hype_check: 趋势评分高达90.46，单日获星近800，表明其在开发者社区中热度显著，但需观察其长期技术影响力。
named_hotspots.value_check: 作为开源工程实践集合，该项目为AI编码智能体的标准化、可部署提供了实用参考，具有明确的工具属性价值。
named_hotspots.follow_up: 可关注该项目后续的版本迭代、实际落地案例，以及是否衍生出相关开发框架或行业标准。
named_hotspots.evidence: 项目描述明确为'Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents'，高星数与活跃度佐证其社区认可度。

named_hotspots.name: superpowers
named_hotspots.what_happened: GitHub 项目 'obra/superpowers' 登上趋势榜，这是一个智能体技能框架与软件开发方法论项目。
named_hotspots.core_details: 该项目由用户 'obra' 创建，使用 Shell 语言编写。今日新增 1205 颗星，总星数已达 223,537，项目贡献者包括 obra, claude, arittr, clkao, jjshanks。
named_hotspots.ai_take: 该项目定位为 '有效的智能体技能框架与软件开发方法论'，表明其旨在为 AI 智能体（Agent）的开发与应用提供一套系统化的工具和工程实践，可能涉及如何设计、组合和管理 AI 智能体的能力。
named_hotspots.hype_check: 热度较高。单日获星超 1200，总星数超过 22 万，表明其在开发者社区中长期受到广泛关注，当前再次成为热点。
named_hotspots.value_check: 作为高星开源项目，其核心价值在于提供了一套被社区验证的、关于 AI 智能体开发的框架和方法论。对于从事 AI 应用、自动化流程和智能体系统开发的工程师和研究者具有参考和实践价值。
named_hotspots.follow_up: 可关注其 GitHub 仓库的更新、文档以及社区讨论，以深入了解其具体架构、技能定义方式以及如何融入实际软件开发流程。
named_hotspots.evidence: 项目描述为 'An agentic skills framework & software development methodology that works.'，GitHub 趋势数据（今日星数 1205，总星数 223537）支持其受关注度。

named_hotspots.name: MoneyPrinterTurbo
named_hotspots.what_happened: 一个名为MoneyPrinterTurbo的GitHub项目在趋势榜上热度飙升，该项目利用AI大模型实现一键生成高清短视频。
named_hotspots.core_details: 该项目由harry0703等人开发，主要使用Python语言。今日新增星标1471个，总星标数已达84938个，显示出极高的社区关注度。
named_hotspots.ai_take: 该项目将AI大模型能力封装为自动化视频生成工具，降低了短视频创作的技术门槛，是AI在内容生成领域应用平民化的一个典型案例。
named_hotspots.hype_check: 项目名称直白地指向“赚钱”目的，可能吸引大量寻求流量变现的用户，但需注意其实际生成视频的质量、版权合规性以及是否过度炒作“一键暴富”概念。
named_hotspots.value_check: 核心价值在于整合了文本到视频的AI生成流程，为内容创作者、营销人员或初学者提供了一个快速原型工具。但其长期价值取决于生成内容的独特性、可控性以及对不同平台规则的适应性。
named_hotspots.follow_up: 关注其后续版本更新是否解决多语言支持、更精细的编辑控制、版权素材合规性等问题，并观察其衍生的实际应用案例与潜在的法律或平台政策风险。
named_hotspots.evidence: 项目描述明确指出“利用AI大模型，一键生成高清短视频”，GitHub趋势数据（日增星标1471，总星标84938）证实了其当前的高热度。

named_hotspots.name: AI工具系统提示词与模型仓库
named_hotspots.what_happened: 一个名为“system-prompts-and-models-of-ai-tools”的GitHub仓库在趋势榜上热度飙升，今日获397星，总星数已达139,492。
named_hotspots.core_details: 该仓库由用户x1xhlol等人维护，旨在收集和整理众多AI编程与开发工具（如Cursor、Devin AI、Replit、VSCode Agent等）的系统提示词、内部工具及AI模型信息，并包含部分开源内容。
named_hotspots.ai_take: 该项目反映了开发者社区对理解和复用主流AI工具内部工作机制的强烈需求，通过集中披露系统提示词，可能降低AI工具的使用门槛并激发定制化开发。
named_hotspots.hype_check: 高星数和趋势排名表明其在开发者中关注度极高，但内容主要为信息聚合，而非原创技术突破。
named_hotspots.value_check: 为开发者提供了宝贵的参考资源，有助于快速理解不同AI工具的设计逻辑与优化方法，具备较高的实用与学习价值。
named_hotspots.follow_up: 关注该仓库的持续更新，以及社区是否会基于这些提示词衍生出新的工具或最佳实践。
named_hotspots.evidence: GitHub趋势数据：今日397星，总星139,492；仓库描述中列举了Cursor、Devin AI、Replit、VSCode Agent等大量AI工具名称。

named_hotspots.name: Tolaria
named_hotspots.what_happened: Tolaria 是一个用于管理 Markdown 知识库的桌面应用程序，在 GitHub 上趋势热度显著，今日获得 618 颗星，总星数达 14861。
named_hotspots.core_details: 该项目由 refactoringhq 组织开发，主要贡献者包括 LucaRonin、github-actions、claude、evolanakis 和 riipandi。使用 TypeScript 语言编写，旨在提供一个桌面环境下的 Markdown 知识库管理工具。
named_hotspots.ai_take: 项目贡献者中包含 'claude'，可能暗示了 AI 辅助开发或自动化流程的集成，反映了 AI 工具在软件开发中的辅助作用趋势。
named_hotspots.hype_check: 趋势分数为 79.16，今日星数增长突出，表明在开发者社区中关注度快速上升，但需观察其长期活跃度与用户采纳情况。
named_hotspots.value_check: 作为开源 Markdown 知识库管理工具，它满足了开发者对本地化、结构化知识管理的需求，具有实用价值，但需评估其与现有工具（如 Obsidian、Typora）的差异化优势。
named_hotspots.follow_up: 关注其版本更新频率、社区讨论（如 Issues 和 Pull Requests）以及是否集成 AI 功能以增强知识管理体验。
named_hotspots.evidence: 数据来源于 GitHub Trending 页面，显示项目语言为 TypeScript，今日星数 618，总星数 14861，贡献者列表包含 AI 相关名称。

named_hotspots.name: Maigret
named_hotspots.what_happened: 一个名为Maigret的开源Python项目在GitHub趋势榜上热度上升，单日获得261颗星，总星数已达31963。该项目旨在通过用户名从超过3000个网站收集个人档案信息。
named_hotspots.core_details: Maigret是一个用Python编写的开源情报（OSINT）工具，允许用户输入一个用户名，自动从数千个网站（如社交媒体、论坛等）搜索并汇总与该用户名相关的公开信息，从而构建个人数字足迹档案。项目由开发者soxoj主导维护，并得到社区贡献。
named_hotspots.ai_take: 虽然Maigret本身不直接使用AI技术，但其大规模数据聚合与模式匹配功能与AI驱动的开源情报分析工具在逻辑上相似。未来可结合自然语言处理（NLP）或机器学习对收集的信息进行自动化分析、去重或风险评估，提升效率。
named_hotspots.hype_check: 项目因实用性和隐私关注引发讨论，热度部分源于公众对数字隐私的担忧及安全研究需求。但需注意，工具可能被滥用，且其数据来源依赖于网站公开性，并非所有信息都准确或最新。
named_hotspots.value_check: 对安全研究人员、渗透测试者和数字隐私意识强的用户具有实用价值，可用于评估个人网络暴露程度。但普通用户需谨慎使用，避免侵犯他人隐私或违反法律。开源模式促进了工具透明度和社区改进。
named_hotspots.follow_up: 关注项目更新以了解新支持的网站和功能改进；可探索与AI分析工具的集成可能性；注意相关法律与伦理指南，确保合规使用。
named_hotspots.evidence: 项目GitHub页面显示描述为“通过用户名从3000+网站收集个人档案”，语言为Python，总星数31963，单日增长261星，由soxoj等维护。

source_briefs.item_name: The theory taking the rich by storm: China funds data center haters
source_briefs.content_summary: 美国内政部长道格·伯古姆在一个活动中表示，美国国内新出现的数据中心反对浪潮并非自发和本地性的，部分资金来源于“外国黑钱”。一些科技界人士，如All-In播客的主持人们，也怀疑存在外国影响，并特别指向中国。
source_briefs.ai_comment: 该报道反映了美国部分政商界人士将国内基础设施建设的争议与地缘政治挂钩的叙事，需注意其信息来源和立场。

source_briefs.item_name: /dmg – a Claude Code skill for persistent memory and session sync
source_briefs.content_summary: 这是一个为Claude Code设计的斜杠命令技能，旨在解决会话间上下文重置的问题。它能在每次会话结束时，自动提交未保存的代码仓库更改、刷新文档并更新持久化记忆系统，从而让下一次会话开始时能获得完整的项目上下文。
source_briefs.ai_comment: 该工具直击当前AI编码助手在长周期、多会话项目协作中的痛点——上下文丢失，是提升开发效率的重要尝试。

source_briefs.item_name: Show HN: Llmbuffer – Python library for cache-optimized LLM conversation history
source_briefs.content_summary: 这是一个Python库，用于优化LLM对话历史的缓存。它通过智能地组织消息顺序（将稳定的系统提示和已提交的对话历史作为不变前缀，将频繁变化的内容如RAG结果放在末尾），最大化缓存复用率，从而显著降低API调用成本和延迟。作者表示，在其智能体应用中实现了超过90%的令牌缓存命中率。
source_briefs.ai_comment: 在LLM应用成本优化方面提供了一个精巧的技术方案，关注底层提示工程和缓存机制，对构建高效、经济的AI应用具有实用价值。

source_briefs.item_name: The theory taking the rich by storm: China funds data center haters - NPR
source_briefs.content_summary: NPR头条报道了“中国资助数据中心反对者”这一在美国富裕阶层和部分政界流传的理论。相关报道汇总了多方说法，包括称中国相关操盘手利用ChatGPT影响美国关于AI数据中心和关税的辩论，以及OpenAI声称中国发起影响行动以塑造美国对AI数据中心的态度等。
source_briefs.ai_comment: 此新闻事件凸显了在AI基础设施（如数据中心）建设竞赛中，舆论战和信息战已成为大国博弈的新前沿。

source_briefs.item_name: Live updates: US military says it fired on a tanker trying to transport oil from Iran - AP News
source_briefs.content摘要: 美联社头条聚焦美军称其袭击了一艘试图从伊朗运输石油的油轮事件。相关报道汇总了美军对伊朗多个目标发动新打击、特朗普誓言要“强硬”打击伊朗等动态。
source_briefs.ai_comment: 地缘政治紧张局势升级，可能对全球能源市场和科技供应链的稳定性产生影响。

source_briefs.item_name: Judge rejects watchdog's bid to block Trump administration's $1.8B 'anti-weaponization' fund - AP News
source_briefs.content_summary: 法官驳回了监督组织试图阻止特朗普政府18亿美元“反武器化”基金的请求。尽管司法部承诺终止该基金，但相关法律挑战仍在继续，法官在拒绝采取行动的同时也对司法部提出了警告。
source_briefs.ai_comment: 此事涉及美国国内政治与法律程序的博弈，其资金用途可能与技术监管等领域相关，值得后续关注。

summary.closing_note: 今日简报揭示了AI开源生态的活力，智能体技能框架成为开发者关注的核心。这些项目不仅提供了即用的工具，更在定义AI与人类协作的新工作流。随着工程化能力的提升，AI正从实验性助手转向可靠的生产力组件。请持续关注这些高热度项目的后续发展，它们很可能塑造未来AI应用开发的标准与实践。
